37→class FakeLocalGitHub(LocalGitHub): 38→ """In-memory fake implementation of GitHub operations. 39→ 40→ This class has NO public setup methods. All state is provided via constructor 41→ using keyword arguments with sensible defaults (empty dicts). 42→ """ 43→ 44→ def __init__( 45→ self, 46→ *, 47→ repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, 48→ prs: dict[str, PullRequestInfo] | None = None, 49→ pr_bases: dict[int, str] | None = None, 50→ pr_details: dict[int, PRDetails] | None = None, 51→ prs_by_branch: dict[str, PRDetails] | None = None, 52→ workflow_runs: list[WorkflowRun] | None = None, 53→ workflow_runs_by_node_id: dict[str, WorkflowRun] | None = None, 54→ run_logs: dict[str, str] | None = None, 55→ pr_plan_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]] | None = None, 56→ polled_run_id: str | None = None, 57→ authenticated: bool = True, 58→ auth_username: str | None = "test-user", 59→ auth_hostname: str | None = "github.com", 60→ issues_gateway: GitHubIssues | None = None, 61→ issues_data: list[IssueInfo] | None = None, 62→ pr_titles: dict[int, str] | None = None, 63→ pr_bodies_by_number: dict[int, str] | None = None, 64→ pr_diffs: dict[int, str] | None = None, 65→ pr_changed_files: dict[int, list[str]] | None = None, 66→ merge_should_succeed: bool = True,

117→ resolve_thread_failures: Set of thread IDs that should fail when resolved 118→ pr_diff_error: If set, get_pr_diff() raises RuntimeError with this message. 119→ Use to simulate HTTP 406 "diff too large" errors. 120→ workflow_runs_error: If set, get_workflow_runs_by_node_ids() raises 121→ RuntimeError with this message. Use to simulate API failures. 122→ artifact_download_callback: Optional callback invoked when download_run_artifact() 123→ is called. Callback receives (run_id, artifact_name, destination) and can create 124→ files in destination to simulate artifact content. Return True for success, 125→ False or raise to simulate failure. 126→ plan_pr_details: Pre-configured data for list_plan_prs_with_details(). 127→ Tuple of (pr_details_list, pr_linkages, unenriched_count). Create GitHub-related classes (need repo_info, local_config) 299→ # Create issues first, then compose into github 300→ # Use plans_repo for cross-repo plan management if configured 301→ issues: GitHubIssues = RealGitHubIssues(target_repo=local_config.github_repo, time=time) 302→ github: LocalGitHub = RealLocalGitHub(time, repo_info, issues=issues) 303→ 304→ plan_store: PlanBackend = PlannedPRBackend(github, issues, time=RealTime()) 305→ plan_list_service: PlanListService = PlannedPRPlanListService(github, time=time) 306→ 307→ # Objectives use GitHub issues (not draft PRs) 308→ objective_list_service: ObjectiveListService = RealObjectiveListService(github, time=time) 309→

95→ remote_url = git.remote.get_remote_url(repo_root, "origin") 96→ owner, name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url) 97→ repo_info = RepoInfo(owner=owner, name=name) 98→ except (ValueError, subprocess.CalledProcessError): 99→ # Not in a git repo, no origin remote, or URL unparseable - repo_info stays None 100→ pass 101→ 102→ # Create issues first, then compose into github 103→ time = RealTime() 104→ issues = RealGitHubIssues(target_repo=None, time=time) 105→ github = RealLocalGitHub(time, repo_info, issues=issues) 106→ graphite = resolve_graphite(RealErkInstallation()) 107→ graphite_branch_ops = ( 108→ RealGraphiteBranchOps() if not isinstance(graphite, GraphiteDisabled) else None 109→ )

45→ 46→ result = subprocess.run( 47→ ["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"], 48→ capture_output=True, 49→ text=True, 50→ check=False, 51→ ) 52→ repo_root = Path(result.stdout.strip()) if result.returncode == 0 else Path.cwd() 53→ 54→ repo_info = get_repo_info(git, repo_root) 55→ time = RealTime() 56→ github_issues = RealGitHubIssues(target_repo=None, time=time) 57→ github: LocalGitHub = RealLocalGitHub(time=time, repo_info=repo_info, issues=github_issues) 58→ 59→ return ErkDevContext(git=git, github=github, repo_root=repo_root) 60→

50→ """ 51→ self.time = RealTime() 52→ self.git = RealGit() 53→ 54→ # Compute repo_info from cwd 55→ repo_root = self.git.repo.get_repository_root(cwd) 56→ repo_info = get_repo_info(self.git, repo_root) 57→ 58→ # Create issues first, then compose into github 59→ issues = RealGitHubIssues(target_repo=None, time=self.time) 60→ self.github = RealLocalGitHub(time=self.time, repo_info=repo_info, issues=issues) 61→ self.graphite = RealGraphite() 62→

session.assistant:
**Phase 5: Wire into context helpers**
The plan says to add `require_actions()` but since both methods on GitHubActions are accessed via `ctx.github.actions`, a simpler approach is to just use `require_github(ctx).actions`. Let me check how the command currently gets its dependencies:
Looking at the command, it uses `require_cwd(ctx)` and `run_subprocess_with_context` directly. After refactoring, it will use `require_github(ctx).actions`. So `require_actions` isn't strictly needed — but the plan asks for it. Let me add it following the existing pattern:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.actions.abc import GitHubActions from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues

session.tool_result: The file /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-16/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py has been updated successfully.
session.assistant: Now add `require_actions` after `require_github`:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
return ctx.obj.github
def require_cwd(ctx: click.Context) -> Path:

session.tool_result: The file /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-16/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py has been updated successfully.
session.assistant: The `context_for_test()` already creates a `FakeLocalGitHub` which now has `actions_gateway` defaulting to `FakeGitHubActions()`, so tests that don't need specific actions data will work automatically. Tests that need specific actions data can pass `github=FakeLocalGitHub(actions_gateway=FakeGitHubActions(...))`.
